steps.title: Identify what the claim requires
steps.content: The passage states that Henry does not always provide comfort to Jimmie. The correct quotation must show Henry refusing or withholding comfort — not giving it, and not doing something unrelated. Look for language of rejection or criticism directed at Jimmie.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'For one thing, Jimmie was invariably overcome with reverence when he was told how beautifully a harness was polished or a horse groomed.': Describes Jimmie's admiration for Henry's work, not Henry's response to Jimmie's shame. Does not address comfort. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Henry... could usually provide a sin to place himself on a footing with the disgraced one.': Shows Henry typically offering solidarity (a form of comfort) — the opposite of what the claim requires. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Henry would sometimes choose to absolutely repudiate [Jimmie's behavior], and when Jimmie appeared in his shame would bully him most virtuously...': 'Absolutely repudiate' and 'bully' show Henry withholding comfort and doing the opposite — scolding Jimmie instead. Directly illustrates the claim. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Whenever Henry dwelt for a time in sackcloth, Jimmie did not patronize him at all.': Describes Jimmie's behavior toward Henry when Henry feels guilty, not Henry's response to Jimmie's shame. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
